concerns.text: The plan cites 'PolyU-COMP-Information' (URL: .../PolyU-COMP-Information.csv) as the source for Total Solar Irradiance (TSI) satellite data. This dataset is unrelated to solar physics (likely university administrative data) and cannot serve as the outcome variable for training the TSI reconstruction model. The plan acknowledges this mismatch but proposes a 'synthetic data' fallback for the core training phase. Training a scientific model on synthetic data derived from the very relationship being investigated (GSN -> TSI) invalidates the research question regarding 'cycle-to-cycle variability' and 'generalization' (US-1), as the model will simply learn the synthetic generator's noise rather than real physical variance. A real, verified TSI satellite dataset (e.g., SORCE/TIM, PMOD) is required to proceed.

The CMIP6 dataset URL points to 'test-00000-of-00012.parquet' within a generic 'wip_cmip6' repository. While CMIP6 is a valid source for climate model baselines, this specific URL appears to be a placeholder or a test fragment rather than a canonical, verified dataset containing the specific TSI or solar forcing variables needed for the 'Baseline Comparison' (US-3). The plan must cite a specific, complete CMIP6 experiment or variable (e.g., 'historical' run, 'solar forcing' variable) with a verified, stable URL to ensure the comparison is scientifically valid.
